steps.title: Spot the sentence parts around the blank
steps.content:
To the left of the blank is the adjective American, which modifies the noun that follows.
To the right is the verb phrase may be best known…, so the blank must supply a noun (or noun phrase) that acts as the head of the subject.

steps.title: Choose the punctuation that fits a restrictive appositive
steps.content:
The correct phrasing is drummer Dennis Chambers with no commas, making the name a restrictive appositive to the noun drummer.
The other three either misplace commas around the name or insert a comma before the verb, breaking the subject‑verb connection.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
